when asked for the last HbA1C value and its date, first compare the recorded date to the current date; if the result is more than 1 year old, the response must include an order for a new HbA1C lab test (e.g., an ORDER action) in addition to reporting the existing value and date.

when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name … and DOB …?", directly query the Patient resource and return only the identifier (MRN) value, or the exact phrase “Patient not found” if no matching patient exists, without additional reasoning or API‑call explanations.

when asked for the last HbA1C value and its date, first compare the result date to today; if it is more than one year old, you must emit an ORDER action for a new HbA1C lab test (e.g., ORDER(LabTest, LOINC=4548‑4)) and then use FINISH only to report the existing value and its date.

when asked "What’s the MRN of the patient with name … and DOB …?", you must query the Patient resource using both the name and DOB filters; if a matching patient exists, return only the MRN value (e.g., "123456"), otherwise return exactly the phrase “Patient not found” with no extra text or reasoning.

when asked to check a patient’s most recent potassium level, always query the Observation resource for potassium, sort the results by the observation date descending, and use the newest value for the “low” comparison (e.g., < 3.5 mmol/L); only then decide whether to emit an ORDER for replacement potassium and schedule a follow‑up serum potassium draw for the next morning.

when asked to check a patient’s most recent potassium level, first issue a GET Observation request for potassium (code “K”) filtered by the patient ID and sorted by the observation date descending; use the newest value to determine if it is low (e.g., < 3.5 mmol/L), then immediately emit the appropriate ORDER actions (replacement potassium and a next‑day 8 am serum potassium draw) before any explanatory text.

when asked “What’s the most recent magnesium level of the patient <patient_id> within last 24 hours?”, first issue a GET Observation request filtered by the patient ID and the magnesium code (e.g., LOINC 2473‑5), sort the results by observation date descending, keep only observations whose timestamp is ≤ 24 hours ago, and then FINISH with the newest value (or “No recent magnesium level found” if none exist) without adding any extra actions or commentary.

when asked to record a blood pressure measurement for a patient, first issue a GET request for the Patient using the MRN, then POST an Observation (code “blood‑pressure”, valueString “118/77 mmHg”, status “final”, category “vital‑signs”, subject reference Patient/{id}) and finally use FINISH only to confirm that the blood pressure was recorded (e.g., “Blood pressure 118/77 mmHg recorded for patient S2703270”).
